title: Screening and Prostate-Cancer Mortality in a Randomized European Study (ERSPC)

1a.note: Title says 'Randomized European Study' — sufficient for CONSORT

1b.note: Structured abstract with background, methods, results, conclusions

2a.note: Introduction describes PSA screening rationale and evidence gap

2b.note: Goal is clear from context but not stated as explicit hypothesis

3b.note: Changes to screening in some centers, age criteria changes described

4a.note: Men 55-69, European countries, eligibility criteria clear

4b.note: 7 countries described, sufficient for multicenter trial

5.note: PSA screening vs no screening — interventions described

6a.note: Primary outcome: rate of death from prostate cancer

6b.note: No mention of changes to outcomes after trial start

7a.note: Power calculation mentioned with sample size determination

7b.note: Interim analyses and DSMC described with alpha spending

8a.note: Random-number generators mentioned but procedures varied by center — not fully detailed

8b.note: Stratification mentioned, but no blocks; procedures differed across centers

9.note: Population registries and random assignment described, but allocation concealment mechanism not fully explicit

10.note: Some info on who enrolled/assigned, but not complete for all centers

11a.note: No explicit statement about open-label design; only blinded outcome assessment mentioned

11b.note: Not applicable for screening trial (screening vs no screening), but not stated

12a.note: Poisson regression, rate ratios, stratification by center and age

12b.note: Subgroup analyses by center, age group, and compliance described

13a.note: 182,160 randomized, numbers per group clear with flow diagram

13b.note: Exclusions mentioned but reasons for losses not fully detailed

14a.note: Recruitment dates and follow-up period (median 9 years) described

14b.note: Data cut-off Dec 2006, trial ongoing — stopping rationale described

15.note: Table 1 with baseline demographics per group

16.note: Numbers in each analysis clearly stated

17a.note: Rate ratios with 95% CI for primary and secondary outcomes

17b.note: Absolute and relative effect sizes (rate ratio 0.80, absolute rates)

18.note: Subgroup analyses by age, center, compliance

19.note: Overdiagnosis and overtreatment discussed as harms

22.note: Balanced interpretation: 20% mortality reduction vs overdiagnosis cost

25.note: Funding sources and role of funders described
